title: When Analyzing Behavior: - Ask for Others:
subtitle: See Beyond Personality
description:
When analyzing behavior:
- Ask for others: "What situations might have influenced their actions?"
- Ask for yourself: "Am I underestimating my own tendencies in this scenario?"
- Broaden the lens: Balance personality traits with situational influences for everyone.

Example: If someone is late, consider traffic or unexpected delays, not just lack of punctuality.

howTo.title: Identify Situational Influences
howTo.summary: When judging someone's behavior, pause to consider what situational factors may have influenced their actions. List at least two possible scenarios that could explain their behavior.

howTo.title: Estimate Plausibility
howTo.summary: Assign a plausibility percentage to each situational hypothesis you generate. This helps you remain open to new information and reduces the tendency to jump to conclusions.

howTo.title: Choose a Low-Escalation Action
howTo.summary: Decide on a neutral, low-escalation response based on your situational hypotheses. This could involve sending a brief message or waiting before reacting.

howTo.title: Log Your Insights
howTo.summary: After applying the habit, take a moment to record your observations and the actions taken. This reinforces learning and helps track progress.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to monitor your progress and refine your approach. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/attribution-bias-checker/.
